verdicts.note: The record claims exists='yes' and points to https://docs.ecwid.com/api-reference, but that URL is the Ecwid REST API reference documentation, NOT a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server. There is no evidence of a dedicated MCP server for Ecwid in the provided materials. The flags mention third-party MCP integrations but 'no official first-party Ecwid MCP server confirmed.'
The URL provided (https://docs.ecwid.com/api-reference) is the Ecwid REST API reference, not an MCP server repository or implementation. This should be removed.

Evidence confirms OAuth2 flow for public apps and secret token access for custom single-store apps copied from the app dashboard. The March 2025 changelog confirms Bearer token in Authorization header is now the only supported method (query param tokens disabled).

The support page (https://support.ecwid.com/hc/en-us/articles/207100729-Ecwid-plans-and-features) indicates REST API access requires a paid plan. The self_serve_notes mention developers can request a free upgrade via email, which matches the 'paid-plan' designation with caveats.
The API reference page confirms a broad REST API v3 covering products, orders, customers, inventory, store profile, categories, discounts, etc.
'buildable-with-caveats' is appropriate given that API access requires either a paid plan or a manual email request to support for a free developer upgrade. This is not fully self-serve.
The main blocker correctly identifies that API access requires a paid plan or manual developer upgrade request, limiting pure self-serve onboarding.
The one_liner accurately summarizes the REST API v3, auth methods (OAuth2 and secret tokens), and the paid plan requirement with developer upgrade option.
All four evidence URLs are present in the provided evidence and support the claims they are associated with.

revised.name: Ecwid
revised.one_liner: Ecwid provides a broad REST API (v3) secured via OAuth2 or dashboard-issued secret tokens, but full API access typically requires a paid plan or a manually-requested developer upgrade.

auth_methods.notes: Standard OAuth 2.0 flow for external/public apps; exchange code for access token via POST to https://my.ecwid.com/api/oauth/token. Bearer token required in Authorization header; query param tokens disabled as of March 2025.
For custom, single-store apps, a pre-generated secret access token can be copied directly from the app dashboard, skipping the OAuth flow.

revised.self_serve_notes: Standard free-tier Ecwid stores cannot access the REST API; a paid plan is normally required. However, developers building integrations/public apps can request a free upgrade to a paid test store by emailing Ecwid API support, after which tokens can be self-generated via the app dashboard.

api_surface.notes: Ecwid REST API v3 covers products, orders, customers, inventory, store profile, categories, discounts, etc. Rate limited to 600 requests per minute per token.

revised.main_blocker: API access requires a paid Ecwid plan (or a manually-requested free developer upgrade via emailing support), which limits pure self-serve onboarding for agent toolkits.

evidence.supports: Describes OAuth2 flow and direct secret token access via app dashboard for custom apps. Confirms no test mode exists and that Bearer token in Authorization header is required.
Confirms Bearer token in Authorization header is now the only supported auth method; query param tokens completely disabled as of March 2025.
Indicates REST API access requires a paid plan on standard tiers.
REST API overview showing broad API surface across store resources including products, orders, customers, categories, discounts, with rate limits of 600 requests per minute per token.

flags: Third-party MCP integrations (viaSocket, Vinkius) exist but no official first-party Ecwid MCP server confirmed.
Free API access for developers requires manual email request to support, not fully self-serve.
No sandbox/test mode exists; must use a real (paid or upgraded) store for testing.
API rate limited to 600 requests per minute per token; exceeding triggers 429 errors with cooldown.
